title: Imperial Reunification
content:

{{Quote|One Empire, one fleet—only this will guarantee us victory.|Admiral [[Natasi Daala|Daala]]|<ref name="Darksaber" />}}
'''Imperial Reunification''' was a [[Politics/Legends|political process]] occurring during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] that resulted in the [[United Warlord Fleets]] in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] and the end of the [[Imperial Civil War]] by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala]] in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]]. 

With the United Warlord Fleets destruction, the Imperial Remnant was established by [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] and sought to topple the New Republic and maintained a strong military and limited venues of public expression, but abolished [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] and anti-[[Alien/Legends|alien]] sentiment. However, it still had limited venues for public expression.

==History==
===Events leading to reunification===
{{Quote|I disdain what these puffed-up, self-appointed warlords have inflicted upon our fighting forces. These squabbling children have caused as much damage to the Empire as the Rebel Alliance has.|[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala|Daala]]|<ref name="Darksaber" />}}
[[File:Daala3.jpg|thumb|left|Admiral Daala c. [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]].]]
Following the death of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had slowly begun to dissolve, as Palpatine did not have a clear succession plan in place. Numerous leaders worked to secure their own power, resulting in the creation of numerous fiefdoms. [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]], a member of Palpatine's inner circle took the throne as regent,<ref name="EGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> but due to his lack of charisma and influence, Pestage was vulnerable and other members of the inner circle formed the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Council]] to oppose him. The [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]], [[Ysanne Isard]], was able to play Pestage and the Ruling Council against each other and, after assassinating some of the members, eventually took control of the Empire for herself.<ref name="TNEC" /> Isard mostly managed to hold the rest of the Empire together for nearly two years before the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] made it a priority to capture [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], which they eventually managed to do during the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|Liberation of Coruscant]] in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].<ref name="EGW" /> With the loss of the capital world, the Empire splintered heavily, with established warlords picking up the pieces that Isard left behind. Isard herself established a small fiefdom based on the world of [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]].<ref name="X:Bacta">''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]''</ref> At this point the central power of the Empire fell to a coalition of Moffs and advisors. However, their power was overshadowed by warlords, most notably Warlord [[Zsinj]].<ref name="CTD">''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]''</ref>

With the return of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], the Empire enjoyed a brief [[Thrawn campaign|period of relative unity]]. During this time, many factions pledged their loyalty to the Grand Admiral.<ref name="Heir to the Empire">''[[Heir to the Empire]]''</ref> However, other factions managed little more than to stop fighting each other during this time. Warlords like Prince-Admiral Krennel lent support by providing personnel and resources. This Imperial peace was in danger of disintegrating following Thrawn's death at the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|Battle of Bilbringi]],<ref name="The Last Command">''[[The Last Command]]''</ref> however, the Imperial Ruling Council returned and united with six Imperial Starfleet commanders to form the [[Imperial Coalition]]. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], the coalition launched an [[Second Battle of Coruscant|all-out assault on Coruscant]], forcing the New Republic government from the planet and into exile.<ref name="Retreat">{{JournalCite|7|Retreat from Coruscant}}</ref> Once it seemed like the Empire might actually win, the infighting started again.

The infighting only stopped when Emperor Palpatine re-emerged in a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body on the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] world of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. With the weaker commanders culled from the herd and the traitors obvious and ready for execution, Palpatine was able to reforge the Empire into a great power.<ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> He spent all his resources building his fleet, most prominently through the use of superweapons. However, his clone host bodies were unstable and deteriorated rapidly. Before he could find a proper host, Palpatine was killed and Byss was destroyed shortly afterwards.<ref name="Dark Empire II">''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]''</ref> The result was the loss of the majority of the rebuilt Imperial fleet and many of its personnel.

In [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], after a [[Daala campaign|brief bout of destruction]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala|Daala]] was forced to retreat to the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]], one of the last remaining Imperial holdings. She worked for months as a freelance diplomat, trying to get the warlords to unite, and if that could not be achieved to at least stop fighting each other. Following an attack by High Admiral [[Treuten Teradoc]] and the death of her first-officer Commander [[Kratas]], Admiral Daala was pressed into the service of Warlord [[Blitzer Harrsk]].<ref name="Darksaber" />

Placed in command of Harrsk's fleet, Daala led the counterattack against Teradoc. However, before the battle could truly begin Daala took personal control of the gunnery station and fired on Warlord Harrsk's flagship, disabling it. She then threatened to blow them both up using her ship's self-destruct. An intrigued Vice Admiral [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], Thrawn's protégé (now leading Teradoc's fleet), who was similarly dissatisfied with the state of the Empire convinced her to retreat with him and discuss a possible alliance.<ref name="Darksaber" />

===The conference at Tsoss Beacon===
{{Quote|I didn't want to rule. I had no intention of becoming a political leader… but you have given me no choice. I cannot leave the Empire in the hands of fools like you.|Admiral Daala at Tsoss Beacon|<ref name="Darksaber" />}}
[[File:GiladPellaeon.jpg|thumb|right|134px|Vice-Admiral Pellaeon]]
Daala and Pellaeon established a meeting place on the Deep Core asteroid of [[Tsoss Beacon/Legends|Tsoss Beacon]] in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], with the hope of convincing the [[Warlord/Legends|Warlords]] to cease their petty rivalries for the good of the Empire. The two convened a conference of the thirteen most powerful Warlords. Among those in attendance were [[Blitzer Harrsk]], [[Treuten Teradoc]], and [[Sander Delvardus]]. Shortly into the meeting, when it became clear that the cease-fire wouldn't last much longer, Daala triggered the doors to slam shut and lock. Even she couldn't unlock them for three standard hours. This forced the Warlords to discuss cooperation for the future of the Empire. They eventually resorted to squabbling over their petty rivalries when they couldn't agree on anything.<ref name="Darksaber" />

Unfortunately, it became clear towards the end of the three hours that there would be no cooperation. Disgusted at the behavior of the remaining Imperial leaders, Daala released a powerful nerve gas which only she and Pellaeon were prepared against. Many of the Warlords rushed to the doors trying to force them open. Superior General Delvardus assembled a blade from his rank insignia, but fell short of killing Daala in his last moments. The last to survive was High Admiral Teradoc, whose bulk kept him planted in his chair and also made the nerve gas take longer to kill him. Shortly after the last of the warlords had apparently died, the doors unlocked and opened, with the three hours now up.<ref name="Darksaber" />

===Daala's reforms===
{{Quote|Imperial forces have been unified, squabbling warlords executed. Starship construction increased tenfold, new soldiers appearing by the tens of thousands…Imperial forces have clustered around a single commander, and it would appear that even women and aliens are allowed to serve to the extent of their abilities—a vast change from the Emperor's way of thinking, wouldn't you say? Charming to see an enlightened Imperial commander, isn't it?|Unnamed [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] information broker|<ref name="Darksaber" />}}
With the death of the warlords, Daala became the [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]], effectively establishing what would become the [[Imperial Remnant]]. She amassed a huge fighting force of millions of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]; 45 [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]], 112 [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]], and one [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Super Star Destroyer]], ''[[Knight Hammer]]''; hundreds of [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]], [[GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat|Skipray Blastboats]], [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]], [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE Bombers]], and [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE Interceptors]]; ''Gamma''-class assault shuttles, and [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttles]]. Many of the vehicles and vessels were newly built from factories she personally toured, and many of the new soldiers were fresh recruits inspired by her charismatic public speaking. Daala's battle group quickly and quietly prepared for a brutal assault on the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

Daala having assumed control over much of the Imperial holdings in the Deep Core, she began reaching out to others with Imperial loyalties beyond. While she didn't achieve solid alliances with many of these worlds at the time, she laid the groundwork for Pellaeon to eventually move the Imperial fleet out of the Deep Core and into the Mid Rim where he launched his own fresh assault against the New Republic.

In addition to military and political consolidation, Daala removed many of the restrictions on women and aliens that had run rampant. For the first time, women and nonhumans made up a large part of the Imperial war machine. This boosted morale and earned Daala the respect and loyalty of many Imperials.

===The Assault on Yavin 4===
{{Main|Assault on Yavin 4}}
Now commanding the "[[United Warlord Fleets]]" out of the Deep Core, Daala began a campaign against the New Republic. The Moffs in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] did not formally ally with her, but supported her logistically and financially. Daala planned to strike first at the growing [[New Jedi Order]] at the [[Jedi Praxeum|Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. To conceal her intentions, she sent [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Ivan Cronus]] with [[Crimson Command]] on a feint to engage in terror raids against targets of opportunity. Cronus [[Attack on Khomm|bombarded Khomm]], [[Battle of the Chardaan Shipyards|destroyed the Chardaan Shipyards]], and [[Attack on Porus Vida|attacked Porus Vida]] while Daala assembled her forces.<ref name="The Essential Atlas" />

However, a successful early campaign was completely undone by a humiliating failure at Yavin 4. Pellaeon arrived in the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]] with a fleet of 17 Star Destroyers with orders to destroy the Praxeum. Daala was only hours behind the vanguard fleet in her Super Star Destroyer ''[[Knight Hammer]]''. Despite [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] being absent, the Jedi Knights defended against the larger enemy force valiantly. [[Kyp Durron]], [[Dorsk 81]], [[Kam Solusar]], [[Tionne Solusar]], and the many other Jedi trainees would fight individual battles with the Imperial forces. However, they realized that they could not hold out forever against such a massive force. Having gathered for a last stand at the [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]], Dorsk 81 suggested to his fellow Jedi that they combine their Force powers similar to what they had done to destroy the spirit of vile Dark Lord [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]]. The powerful blast of Force energy drove Pellaeon's fleet away from Yavin 4 at the cost of Dorsk's life. Meanwhile, the Jedi Knight [[Callista Ming]] set off charges on Admiral Daala's ship, destroying several critical systems. Daala ordered an an immediate mass evacuation and the dying ''Knight Hammer'' was abandoned, falling into the crushing atmosphere of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin Prime]].<ref name="The Essential Atlas" />

==Aftermath==
Daala promoted [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] to full Admiral following her disastrous [[Assault on Yavin 4|defeat]] at [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin]] and surrendered command to him. Pellaeon took the decision to abandon the Deep Core, which he believed could be little more than a refuge for Imperial ideals, and could never support a functioning society. He took the fleet and scoured the abandoned armories for resources, retrieving squadrons of [[TIE/D automated starfighter|droid TIE fighters]], warships, and arms caches. His greatest prize was the most surprising one: the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyers]] ''[[Megador]]'' and [[Dominion (Star Dreadnought)|''Dominion'']], left behind in Harrsk's shipyard [[Deep 3]]. Both Super Star Destroyers became the linchpins of Pellaeon's fleet.<ref name="The Essential Atlas" /><ref name="EGW" /> Much of the infrastructure in the Deep Core remained, however, and [[replacement warlord]]s sprang up to fill the shoes of the men Daala had murdered. These new warlords lacked military muscle but held tight control over their territories. Eventually these replacements wielded enough power that Daala formed them into a coalition to fight the New Republic. However, Daala didn't assume complete control this time and the replacement warlords crumbled under a series of [[Imperial Skirmishes|border assaults]] by the New Republic in the final Imperial campaign.

Pellaeon took what Daala set in motion and formalized the Imperial Remnant, and also expanded upon it, where he established solid alliances with the [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress worlds]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] as part of the establishment of a new and respectable Imperial holding in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] after pulling out the Deep Core. From here, it was easier for him to attack the New Republic and resources were more readily available, and the power of his Super Star Destroyers gave him the confidence to launch the [[Orinda campaign]] later in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]].<ref name="The Essential Atlas" /><ref name="EGW" />

The Imperial Remnant would continue plaguing the Republic until the signing of the [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|Bastion Accords]] on the [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']] in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]].
